families.name: llm-rhythm-changes-heads
families.note: All built on the 32-bar AABA 'I Got Rhythm' changes (usually in Bb): the head melodies differ but the harmonic form (I-vi-ii-V A-sections and a III7-VI7-II7-V7 dominant-cycle bridge) is shared. FIFTY SECOND = Monk's '52nd Street Theme', WEE = 'Allen's Alley'.

families.name: llm-rhythm-changes
families.note: 'WEE' (also known as Allen's Alley, Denzil Best) is a bebop head built on the AABA 'I Got Rhythm' changes; classic rhythm-changes contrafact of I GOT RHYTHM.

families.name: llm-bebop-blues-in-f
families.note: Both are Charlie Parker 12-bar bebop blues heads (in F) sharing the standard bebop-blues progression with ii-V turnarounds.

families.name: llm-thou-swell-versions
families.note: Two arrangement versions of the same Rodgers & Hart tune 'Thou Swell', sharing the same underlying melody/harmony.

llm_modulating_bridge_examples.title: BODY AND SOUL
llm_modulating_bridge_examples.note: Ballad in Db whose bridge lifts to the distant key of D major before returning.

llm_modulating_bridge_examples.title: CHEROKEE
llm_modulating_bridge_examples.note: Bridge famously modulates through a descending sequence of ii-V's (starting in B) far from the home key of Bb.

llm_modulating_bridge_examples.title: HAVE YOU MET MISS JONES
llm_modulating_bridge_examples.note: Bridge cycles through three keys a major third apart (the 'Coltrane changes' bridge) rather than staying in the home key of F.

llm_modulating_bridge_examples.title: GIRL FROM IPANEMA THE
llm_modulating_bridge_examples.note: Bridge shifts to a remote key (a half-step-up tonal area) contrasting the home key of the A-sections.

llm_modulating_bridge_examples.title: ALL THE THINGS YOU ARE
llm_modulating_bridge_examples.note: Continuously modulating form; the bridge tonicizes G major then E major, distinct from the Ab/opening tonal center.

llm_modulating_bridge_examples.title: IN A SENTIMENTAL MOOD
llm_modulating_bridge_examples.note: A-sections in the home key with a bridge that moves to a contrasting key area (Db) a distance away.

llm_modulating_bridge_examples.title: I GOT RHYTHM
llm_modulating_bridge_examples.note: Rhythm-changes bridge leaves the tonic entirely for a III7-VI7-II7-V7 dominant cycle.

llm_modulating_bridge_examples.title: TAKE THE A TRAIN
llm_modulating_bridge_examples.note: A-sections in C with a bridge that moves up to the II (D) tonal area.

llm_modulating_bridge_examples.title: CON ALMA
llm_modulating_bridge_examples.note: Dizzy Gillespie tune whose harmony continually modulates through symmetrical key centers, a strong seed for the modulating-section family.
